message:
fix(runtime): remove pane_activity_tracker entry on PaneDestroyed (ft-l6v1r)

The `NativeEvent::PaneDestroyed` handler in `handle_native_event` and
the wezterm `diff.closed_panes` loop in `ObservationRuntime::run` each
cleaned up `cursors` and `detection_contexts` for the teardown pane
but **not** `pane_activity_tracker`. Every pane ever seen leaked a
`PaneActivityState` into that map for the lifetime of the runtime,
which on a long-running aggregator translates to unbounded growth on
the PaneActivity path and skewed generation counters if a pane_id got
reused (since `last_seq`/`generation` from the dead pane would still
be there when a new pane registered under the same id).

Introduce two helpers:

* `remove_runtime_pane_state(pane_id, &mut cursors, &mut dctx, &mut
  tracker) -> RuntimePaneStateRemoval` — the pure synchronous core
  that removes from all three maps and reports which removals
  actually happened, so tests can pin the invariant directly.

* `remove_runtime_pane_state_for_pane(pane_id, &Arc<RwLock<..>>,
  &Arc<RwLock<..>>, &Arc<RwLock<..>>)` — the async wrapper used by
  both teardown sites. It acquires all three write locks, calls the
  pure helper, and `debug_assert!`s that the pane is gone from every
  map (turning any future regression into an immediate panic in debug
  builds rather than a slow leak in prod).

Both teardown sites now call the same helper, and the
`pane_activity_tracker: &Arc<RwLock<HashMap<u64, PaneActivityState>>>`
handle is plumbed through `handle_native_event`'s signature and the
`tokio::spawn` closure in `run_native` so the async path has it.

Adds `ft_l6v1r_pane_cleanup_removes_destroyed_pane_from_runtime_maps`
— asserts on the synchronous helper that all three maps are pruned
for exactly the target pane and no others.

Also folds a handful of brace-only blocks into single-line form
(`config_update_pending`, `config_take_update`, `runtime_timeout`,
`spawn_runtime_task`, `recv_event`, `send_runtime_channel`,
`persist_captured_segment_for_runtime`, `mpsc_max_capacity`) to match
what rustfmt wants on this tree — no semantic change, pure formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
